evals.prompt: I have an ABAP system at https://dev.acme.corp:44300, client 120. I got this PROPFIND response from Base/baseLib/. Which themes are custom themes vs SAP-provided? The response is in evals/fixtures/propfind_baselib.xml
evals.expected_output: Identifies SAP themes (sap_horizon, sap_horizon_dark, sap_horizon_hcb, sap_horizon_hcw) by their sap_ prefix, identifies custom themes (acme_brand, acme_dark) by their non-sap prefix. Mentions that the authoritative check is reading .theming and checking sVendor.

assertions.description: Correctly identifies acme_brand and acme_dark as custom themes
assertions.check: Response identifies acme_brand and acme_dark as custom/customer themes

assertions.description: Correctly identifies sap_* themes as SAP-provided
assertions.check: Response identifies sap_horizon, sap_horizon_dark, sap_horizon_hcb, sap_horizon_hcw as SAP-provided themes

assertions.description: Skips the first href (the folder itself)
assertions.check: Response does NOT list Base/baseLib/ itself as a theme — it correctly skips the first entry which is the queried folder

assertions.description: Mentions sVendor check for authoritative distinction
assertions.check: Response mentions that checking sVendor in the .theming file is the authoritative way to distinguish custom from SAP themes

evals.prompt: Here's the .theming metadata for our acme_brand theme: evals/fixtures/acme_brand_theming.json. Is this theme up to date or does it need a rebuild? Can I modify it?
evals.expected_output: Identifies that the theme is up to date (sLastBuilt > sLastModified), confirms it is writable (sVendor is CUSTOMER), and notes it extends sap_horizon.

assertions.description: Correctly determines theme is up to date
assertions.check: Response states the theme is up to date / does not need rebuild, because sLastBuilt (2025-04-10T16:46:01) is after sLastModified (2025-04-10T16:45:22)
Response confirms the theme is writable/modifiable because sVendor is CUSTOMER
Response mentions that the theme extends/inherits from sap_horizon

assertions.description: Does NOT suggest the theme is read-only
assertions.check: Response does NOT say the theme is read-only or SAP-provided

evals.prompt: Here's the .theming for sap_horizon: evals/fixtures/sap_horizon_theming.json. I want to change sapBrandColor in this theme. How do I do it?
evals.expected_output: Refuses to modify sap_horizon directly because sVendor is SAP (read-only). Suggests creating a custom theme that extends sap_horizon (via copy) and modifying the parameter there.

assertions.check: Response explains that sap_horizon cannot be modified because sVendor is SAP
Response suggests creating/copying a custom theme based on sap_horizon to make the change

assertions.description: Does NOT provide modification commands for the SAP theme
assertions.check: Response does NOT provide curl PUT commands to modify sap_horizon directly

evals.prompt: I want to change sapBrandColor to #e63946 in our acme_brand theme. The theme's .theming is at evals/fixtures/acme_brand_theming.json and the parent sap_horizon's base.less is at evals/fixtures/sap_horizon_base.less. Environment: SAP_ABAP_HOST=https://dev.acme.corp:44300, SAP_ABAP_CLIENT=120. Generate the curl commands I need to run.
evals.expected_output: Generates curl commands to: (1) GET the current base.less of acme_brand, (2) modify @sapBrandColor value, (3) fetch CSRF token, (4) PUT the modified base.less, (5) PUT updated .theming with new timestamp, (6) send ThemeSaved notification. Uses xsrfid header (not X-CSRF-Token) and correct URL structure.

assertions.check: Response targets the file at Base/baseLib/acme_brand/base.less (the base file in the custom theme, not the parent)
Response uses -H "xsrfid: ..." (not X-CSRF-Token) in write requests
Response PUTs the LESS file before PUTting the .theming metadata
Response mentions updating mData.mThemeMasterState.mSourceInfo.sLastModified to the current timestamp
Response includes a Notify request with ThemeSaved event for acme_brand
Response URLs use the pattern https://dev.acme.corp:44300/sap/bc/theming/themes/~client-120/...
Response includes a CSRF token fetch step (GET/HEAD with X-CSRF-Token: Fetch) before the write operations

evals.prompt: I want to add custom CSS to our acme_brand theme. I need to style .acme-header with a gradient background using sapBrandColor and a bottom border using sapGroup_ContentBorderColor. Where do I put this CSS and what's the correct file format?
evals.expected_output: Directs the user to write in UR/ls/acme_brand/custom.less (not Base/baseLib/custom.less which is for palette parameters). Shows the LESS content wrapped in /*<SAP_FREETEXT_LESS>*/ markers, referencing parameters with @ syntax.

assertions.description: Targets UR/ls or UI5/sap/ui/core framework custom.less
assertions.check: Response targets UR/ls/acme_brand/custom.less or UI5/sap/ui/core/themes/acme_brand/custom.less as the file to edit (NOT Base/baseLib/acme_brand/custom.less)
Response wraps the custom CSS content in /*<SAP_FREETEXT_LESS>*/ and /*</SAP_FREETEXT_LESS>*/ markers
Response uses @sapBrandColor and @sapGroup_ContentBorderColor with LESS @ syntax (not CSS var())
Response explains or implies that Base/baseLib/custom.less is for palette parameters, not visual CSS rules

evals.prompt: I want to delete the acme_dark theme from our system. I know it exists in Base/baseLib/ and some UI5 libraries. What's the full procedure? Don't actually run anything, just describe the steps and generate example commands. Environment: SAP_ABAP_HOST=https://dev.acme.corp:44300, SAP_ABAP_CLIENT=120.
evals.expected_output: Describes the full multi-framework deletion: verify sVendor is CUSTOMER, discover all framework/library occurrences via PROPFIND, fetch CSRF token, DELETE each occurrence with Depth: infinity, optionally notify with ThemeDeleted event, verify deletion.

assertions.check: Response includes a step to verify sVendor is CUSTOMER (not SAP) before proceeding with deletion
Response describes discovering/enumerating multiple frameworks (at minimum mentions Base and UI5, possibly UR and NWBC)
Response explains that the theme must be deleted from ALL library folders across all frameworks, not just Base/baseLib/
Response includes -H "Depth: infinity" in the DELETE curl command
Response mentions sending a ThemeDeleted notification event after deletion

assertions.description: Confirms with user before destructive action
assertions.check: Response mentions confirming with the user or asking for confirmation before proceeding with deletion

evals.prompt: I want to define a palette parameter @acmePrimaryBlue that derives from sapBrandColor with 10% darkening, and @acmeSubtleGray as a 5% fade of sapFontColor. Where do these go and what are the rules?
evals.expected_output: Directs to Base/baseLib/acme_brand/custom.less (NOT custom_css.less). Shows LESS variable definitions using darken() and fade() functions within SAP_FREETEXT_LESS markers. Mentions that palette parameter names must NOT start with 'sap'.

assertions.check: Response targets Base/baseLib/acme_brand/custom.less (or equivalent) as the file for palette parameters
Response uses darken(@sapBrandColor, 10%) and fade(@sapFontColor, 5%) or equivalent LESS function syntax
Response shows the parameters wrapped in /*<SAP_FREETEXT_LESS>*/ markers
Response mentions that palette parameter names must NOT start with 'sap' (the prefix is reserved)
Response does NOT direct the user to custom_css.less (that file is auto-generated merged CSS, not for palette parameters)

evals.prompt: I want to duplicate sap_horizon to create a new theme called acme_horizon. What steps are involved? Don't execute anything. Environment: SAP_ABAP_HOST=https://dev.acme.corp:44300, SAP_ABAP_CLIENT=120.
evals.expected_output: Describes the full copy workflow: discover all library folders containing sap_horizon, COPY each to acme_horizon, update .theming metadata (sId, sLabel, sVendor to CUSTOMER), update LESS files to replace sap_horizon references with acme_horizon, send ThemeSaved notification.

assertions.check: Response explains that the copy must happen across all framework/library locations (not just Base/baseLib/)

assertions.description: Updates sId in .theming to new name
assertions.check: Response mentions updating sId to acme_horizon in the .theming metadata
Response mentions setting sVendor to CUSTOMER in the copy's .theming

assertions.description: Updates LESS files with new theme ID
assertions.check: Response mentions updating LESS source files to replace references to the source theme ID with the new theme ID
Response uses or mentions the WebDAV COPY HTTP method for copying folders
Response includes sending a ThemeSaved notification for the new theme after copying

evals.prompt: I want to list the custom themes on our ABAP system. Can you show me what's available?
evals.expected_output: Detects that SAP_ABAP_HOST, SAP_ABAP_CLIENT, and/or SAP_ABAP_AUTH are not set. Guides the user to set these environment variables before proceeding, explains what each one is for, and shows how to construct SAP_ABAP_AUTH (without asking for raw passwords in the conversation).

assertions.check: Response asks the user to provide or set SAP_ABAP_HOST (the system URL)
Response asks the user to provide or set SAP_ABAP_CLIENT (the SAP client number)
Response asks the user to provide or set SAP_ABAP_AUTH (the authorization header value)

assertions.description: Explains how to construct SAP_ABAP_AUTH safely
assertions.check: Response shows how to construct the auth value (e.g. Basic + base64 encoding) and instructs the user to do this themselves, NOT to paste their password into the conversation

assertions.description: Does NOT attempt to run curl without the env vars
assertions.check: Response does NOT generate curl commands that reference unset $SAP_ABAP_HOST or proceed as if the variables are available
